updated: 2026-07-17 (M4 Part 5 — T49: infection as staged identity). Infection stops being a countdown to an instant Game Over and becomes an IDENTITY read only from symptoms (FR-INJ-05/06/07/08 · GDD Part VI). Stages none→incubating(asymptomatic)→symptomatic→advanced→terminal, hidden progression NEVER shown. The crux (FR-INJ-08): reaching terminal no longer ends the run — it opens the cure race; the run ends by infection only at a delayed succumb (progression ≥148, ~a day past terminal onset) reached by neglecting the race (the authored death scene stays T62). Perception distorts at advanced/terminal (FR-INJ-06): hallucinated leads + memory gaps via a STATELESS cyrb128 hash (pure render, no rng advance, resume-safe), suppressed beside any real threat and never fabricating one; the honest 'can't trust your senses' warning rides the always-shown status at advanced AND terminal so distrusting the story channel is fair; NPCs react to the visible sign and a visibly-dying player can't recruit; stage-scaled fever fatigue makes the run harder. FR-INJ-07: diagnose (precise stage — the opt-in clarity valve) + treat-infection (antibiotics cure, strong/certain early, stage-scaled + seeded-uncertain late via a new 'infection' RNG stream) + quarantine (shelter clean-conditions, strong early, not offered at terminal), each with an honest no-number outcome; all gated on symptomatic+ so the asymptomatic clock stays hidden. New schema-validated content type content/infections/ (gate 8→9 types) mirrors the engine dials under a harness drift guard. ONE save rung v8→v9 re-derives infection.stage under the new bands. Adversarial 2-subagent audit → 7 fixes (quarantine dead-option trap, director distress missing 'advanced', quarantine rest bonus, hallucination-vs-real-threat, terminal perception-warning, cure feedback, stronger comprehension gate). CI green in a clean sandbox: engine 449 · content-loader 9 · harness 85 · typecheck ×3 · schema gate 128 entries / 9 types · malformed rejected · empty-turn smoke exit 0. Every prior bite-free golden byte-identical; 14-stage order unchanged. Retires the 'infection confusion' tripwire. currentMilestone stays M4 (active). See docs/plans/M4_PART5_PLAN.md and docs/qa/QA_REVIEW_M4_PART5.md. ||| PRIOR (M4 Part 4 — T48: the world stops repeating itself). Repeat-suppression & recombination (FR-ENC-01/02 · FR-CNT-04 · the PRD §4 verbatim-repeat gate). T47's by-fit selection gains tagged-pool WEIGHTING + COOLDOWNS so a full run keeps verbatim repeats under §4 (< 5%) by recombination + cooldowns, never raw volume. Selection is now TIERED: an eligible one-shot (scripted) beat still wins deterministically by fit with NO RNG draw — a chain payoff / evolution beat is never randomized or crowded out, so every prior golden stays byte-identical; only an all-repeatable eligible set runs the ambient model (single candidate → no draw; ≥2 → one weighted pick from a new named `encounter` RNG stream favouring fresh + tag-diverse content). The whole anti-repeat store rides the Living History — cooldowns + the §4 metric read the `encounter.begin` beats T47 already logs — so T48 adds NO save-schema rung (stays v8) and the `encounter` stream rides the existing open rng.streams map; the history scan is bounded to the 336h cooldown cap. New content fields tags (recombination/diversity handle) + weight on EncounterDef/encounter.schema.json; cooldownHours now ENFORCED. Instrumentation: engine/telemetry/repetition.ts (mirrors the T32 pacing baseline — pure, client-driven, off by default) → verbatimRepeatRate (a same-id re-fire within a 48h recency window; cooldowns ≥ window drive it toward 0) + distinct/immediate/maxSingleShare/byCategory. Owner chose a HARD CI GATE: a deterministic full-run city sweep asserts fires≥40, rate < 5%, 0 immediate, distinct≥12, maxShare≤25% — MEASURED 134 fires / 54 days ⇒ 0.00% verbatim, 20 distinct, 12.7% max share, across 5 categories (the sweep holds nodes uncontested + claims no safehouse, so combat/shelter are out of its scope by construction). New cadence rule (applyAction): no new encounter opens on a turn spent resolving one (inert for T47). Content: 12 tagged repeatable ambient encounters across 6 of the 7 categories (no ambient combat beat — encounters fire only on uncontested nodes per PL-M4-14) (city-voices ×3, the-dead ×2, living-thing ×2, scavenger-sign ×2, passing-stranger, holding-the-base, night-nerves), each cooldown ≥ 72h; FR-CNT-04 completed by bumping the two 2-choice anchors to ≥3 approaches. Adversarial content-quality subagent audit → fixes (3 HIGH dominance/label breaks restored to real trades, 5 MED weak-trade + near-duplicate re-flavours, LOW cadence tics). CI green in a clean sandbox: engine 418 · content-loader 9 · harness 75 · typecheck ×3 · schema gate 127 entries / 8 types · malformed rejected · empty-turn smoke exit 0. No save-schema rung; 14-stage order unchanged. currentMilestone stays M4 (active). See docs/plans/M4_PART4_PLAN.md and docs/qa/QA_REVIEW_M4_PART4.md.
phase: M4 Content-complete city — breadth without repetition, the full first city. The launch Content Bible for one city: all regions & nodes, the target survivor pool with several companions, full encounter categories with chains & evolution, infection as staged identity, the radio network, a crafting/rooms/jobs economy, factions & inter-NPC relationships, depth screens, an adaptive audio mix with non-audio equivalents, difficulty modes, and the accessibility baseline complete — all content schema-validated in CI. Exit: MVP / public beta. Entry: M3 done and the Slice Fun Gate passed; content schemas frozen for authoring.

tasks.title: Accept ADR-0003 — save storage & versioning
tasks.note: Accepted 2026-07-05: design/decisions/0003-save-storage-versioning.md. Local-first, client-owned I/O of the T7 SaveFile string (harness/native → atomic file under OS app-data; web → IndexedDB, not localStorage); single rolling autosave slot per run written write-temp-then-atomic-rename with a retained .bak (zero-corruption target); saves export/import as text (bug repro, DESIGN §9); migration = T7 forward-only ladder, one integer SAVE_SCHEMA_VERSION bumped only on breaking state-shape change, one pure/total N→N+1 rung per bump; optional cloud sync deferred post-launch as additive transport. tasks.title: Region & node graph with fog-of-war + node memory
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-05: node graph is content — new content/schemas/node.schema.json + content/nodes/ (6 Rivermouth nodes forming a ring) + content/regions/region.rivermouth.json (replaced throwaway test-downtown). Engine prototype/engine/src/map/: buildRegionGraph enforces cross-file integrity JSON Schema can't (symmetric routes, one start, fully connected, known region/node refs) throwing MapError; NodeState gains a `discovered` fog flag (visited = lastVisit!==null); fogOfWar.discoverAround reveals a node + its neighbors; seedWorld.startRun bootstraps a run — seeds regions/nodes from baselines, stands player on start (visited today), reveals fog around it. Pure/dependency-free/integer/plain-JSON, deterministic, save-round-trips. 21 engine tests (integrity failures, reveal, seed, property) + 3 harness integration tests over shipped content. FR-SIM-02, FR-MAP-01..03,06. Unblocks T12 (move/search/rest over the graph).

tasks.title: Core action loop — move / search / rest with time cost
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-05: pipeline stages 1-4 + 14 made real (stages 5-13 still identity). New src/time/clock.ts (phaseOf + advanceClock: hours→day/phase rollover, turn++) and src/actions/coreActions.ts (availableActions/assertLegal/applyPlayerAction/tickNeeds/sceneOf); Action/Scene/SceneChoice extracted to src/pipeline/contract.ts. applyAction(state,action,graph?) now threads the transient graph: validate rejects unoffered actions (IllegalActionError, FR-CORE-01), advanceTime spends each action's hours (move 2/search 3/rest 6) so time always advances (FR-CORE-03), resolvePlayerAction applies move (relocate+visit+fog reveal), search (searchPct+34), rest (fatigue-40); updatePlayer drifts hunger/thirst/fatigue by hours; generateScene renders the Four Questions (FR-CORE-05). wait stays inert (0-cost) so all M0 tests pass unchanged. Pure/deterministic/integer/plain-JSON; save-lossless after every turn (FR-CORE-07). 14 engine loop tests + 2 harness integration turns over shipped Rivermouth. Unblocks T13 (telemetry that every turn changes >=1 system), T14 (noise), T17 (loot yield on search).

tasks.title: Every resolved turn changes ≥1 system (telemetry-audited)
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-05: prototype/engine/src/telemetry/turnAudit.ts — the FR-CORE-04 no-no-op-turn audit, by machine not by hand. jsonEqual (by-value plain-JSON deep-equal, honest where a pure stage re-allocates an identical slice) + diffSystems (which TRACKED_SYSTEMS changed) + auditTurn ({turn,resolved,changedSystems,ok}). TRACKED_SYSTEMS deliberately EXCLUDES meta: counting the always-advancing clock/turn would make the invariant vacuous — the audit asks whether a real system moved beyond time. applyAction now instruments every turn, returning changed[] on TurnResult (empty for an inert wait — no turn resolved). Pure/deterministic/dependency-free. 12 engine tests (jsonEqual, meta-exclusion, per-action change reporting, a fast-check property that every resolved turn over random play changes >=1 system, and a hand-built clock-only turn proving the audit reports ok:false) + a harness 100-turn telemetry audit over shipped Rivermouth (all 100 resolved turns changed >=1 system, zero violations). engine 79 + harness 11 green. Feeds T14 (noise as a per-turn node-memory change) and the T21 Loop-Feel gate.

tasks.title: Noise deposit model
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-05: prototype/engine/src/sim/noise.ts + pipeline stage 6 (updateNode) made real. Each turn decays every node's noise by hours*NOISE_DECAY_PER_HOUR (5/h, floored 0) then deposits the action's noise at the player's current node (their location is already the move destination by stage 6) — decay-before-deposit keeps a fresh sound at full strength for the next turn's read (DESIGN §5 gunshot-this-turn/horde-next-turn; consumer is M2). noiseOf ranks volume: rest/wait 0, move 8, search 25, with an integer params.noise override (the T15 firearm hook). Noise lives in existing NodeState.noise (0-100 int). Pure/deterministic/dependency-free/integer; a silent zero-cost wait returns state untouched (M0 empty-turn contract preserved). DoD proven: the quiet path is legibly quieter (a rummaging run leaves the district materially noisier than a resting one). 14 engine tests + 2 harness integration runs over shipped Rivermouth (loud/quiet A/B autosave-lossless; 30-turn run every resolved turn passes the T13 audit). engine 92 + harness 13 green. Unblocks T15 (firearms deposit region-scale noise; stealth detection reads node noise).

tasks.title: Avoidable turn-based combat + loud firearms + stealth path
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-05: prototype/engine/src/combat/combat.ts + content/enemies/enemy.walker.json + content/schemas/enemies.schema.json. Walkers loiter at a node (new NodeState.walkers, node memory); a contested node offers an avoidable encounter: Fight (melee 1-2 dmg + noise, alerted walker may inflict a named T16 wound), Fire (only if a loaded firearm carried; one-shots a walker, spends a round, dumps FIRE_NOISE 75 >> MELEE_NOISE 15 via the T14 params.noise override, FR-CBT-04), and Slip away to any discovered neighbour (FR-CBT-05 DoD: detection roll over node.noise+phase decides clean escape vs a parting wound, but you always escape — stealth-only survivor crosses the region without ever fighting). A started fight is GameState.combat (nullable), carried across turns so each Strike/Fire/Retreat is one turn-based exchange (FR-CBT-02); a kill clears combat and decrements node.walkers. Every option debits a real resource (FR-CBT-01: time/noise/ammo/wound-risk). All rolls from named RNG streams (combat, stealth) — a seed reproduces a fight. State-shape change: SAVE_SCHEMA_VERSION 1->2 + one forward-only migration rung (old saves gain combat:null + every node walkers:0); combat added to TRACKED_SYSTEMS; stage-1 validation now keys on choiceId so combat actions are validated (bare wait still inert). 12 engine tests + 3 harness integration runs over a hand-built district. engine 112 + harness 16 green; content gate green over enemies+nodes+regions+wounds. Implemented after T16 (combat consumes wounds). Unblocks T17 (loot on a searched/contested node) and T19 (Scene narrates the fight).

tasks.title: Named wounds — treated, not regenerated
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-05: prototype/engine/src/sim/wounds.ts + content/wounds/ (laceration/sprain/bite/fracture) + content/schemas/wound.schema.json (schema-gate validated, Ajv2020 strict). Damage is a discrete named Wound in player.condition.wounds carrying the type id (never a copy), site, severity, accumulated treated, inflictedDay; persists across turns and saves. FR-INJ-04 invariant enforced: inflictWound appends (wounds accumulate, never merge); ONLY treatWound (spending care) advances treated; a wound leaves the body solely when treated>=severity; time/rest/movement never lower a wound. woundBurden (summed untreated remainder) is the handle T15 combat / needs / T19 UI read. Pure/deterministic/dependency-free/integer. 13 engine tests incl. two anti-regen proofs (loop play never lowers a wound; fast-check property the wound multiset never shrinks over no-treatment play). engine 100 green; schema gate green over nodes+regions+wounds. Unblocks T15 (an enemy blow inflicts a named wound). Implemented before T15 in dependency order (combat consumes wounds).

tasks.title: Finite, contested, depleting loot economy
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-05: prototype/engine/src/sim/loot.ts + node `kind` field (content/nodes + node.schema.json). Loot is a finite stock: RegionState.loot (0-100). Searching draws from it and DEBITS it (FR-ECO-01) via a partial, diminishing yield (searchYieldCap — capped by region thinness and node searchPct, FR-ECO-03); the item is drawn from a plausibility table keyed to node kind (medical/store/police/generic/..., FR-ECO-02). Pipeline stage 7 (updateRegion) thins every region each turn by trunc(hours*survivorActivity/50) — rivals contest the same stock (FR-ECO-01), so an unsearched node can be poorer on return. Loot only falls, clamped at 0: a run can never pull more than the region held; a depleted region yields nothing but time+noise. Deterministic (named loot RNG stream), pure/dependency-free/integer. 6 shipped Rivermouth nodes gained a kind. 10 engine tests + 1 harness integration run over shipped Rivermouth (scavenging fills the pack while the stock only falls, lossless, zero audit violations). engine 122 + harness 17 green; content gate green. Feeds T18 (weight forces leave-behind) and T19 (Scene narrates the find).

tasks.title: Weight-limited inventory
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-05: prototype/engine/src/sim/inventory.ts (FR-PLR-03). Carrying is now finite: CARRY_CAPACITY=40 weight budget, per-item ITEM_WEIGHTS (consumables 1 / food+water 3 / bulky 6 / firearm 8; unknown 2), inventoryWeight/remainingCapacity/fits. addItemBounded pockets a find only if it fits (returns {inventory,carried}); dropItem reclaims one unit. Wired into T17 loot: resolveSearchLoot debits the region's finite loot ONLY for what was carried, so a full pack stops draining the well (FR-ECO<->FR-PLR closed). New drop action offered per carried stack in availableActions (0-hour, no clock advance); sceneOf reads the pack ('heavy X/40' / 'full'). Carry weight is DERIVED, not stored -> no SAVE_SCHEMA_VERSION bump, nothing to migrate. Pure/deterministic/dependency-free/integer. 11 engine tests incl. a fast-check property that no search sequence overfills the pack + a full-pack-doesn't-debit-loot proof. Engine 133 + harness 17 green. Feeds T19 (Scene narrates the find/leave-behind).

tasks.title: Lossless quit/resume at any turn boundary → Loop-Feel Check
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-05 (build): prototype/harness/src/play.ts resume seam + src/playCli.ts + test/resume.test.ts. Lossless quit/resume at ANY turn boundary - saveState serializes the T7 SaveFile, resumeSession reconstructs from the string alone (graph rebuilt from content, never stored) and continues; proven byte-identical to an uninterrupted run at EVERY boundary across turn types incl. a mid-combat boundary (combat is in state). New keyboard-only interactive CLI (npm run play / --resume) over a pipe-safe read-render-resolve loop, client-owned save file (ADR-0003). A full M1 slice runs end to end over shipped Rivermouth exercising every Part 1-3 system, every turn passing the T13 audit and lossless each boundary. 4 harness tests. Engine 133 + harness 36 + content-loader 9 green; schema gate green; npm start smoke exit 0. REMAINING: the Loop-Feel Check (PRODUCTION §4 Stage-1) is the owner's human verdict on whether picking an action is interesting - it cannot be auto-passed. Record it in the Fun-Gate log; a pass opens M2.

tasks.title: Loop-feel tuning — survival pressure (needs/wounds/mortality)
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-05: prototype/engine/src/sim/survival.ts. Response to the Loop-Feel read that move/search/rest was frictionless. Needs drift meaningfully (thirst 2/hr, hunger 1/hr, fatigue 2/hr; rest recovers fatigue) and eat/drink spend scavenged food/water to relieve them (why the pack matters). Untreated wounds tire you; a bite drives infection (incubating->symptomatic->terminal) that kills; treat spends the right med (T16 treatWound) to halt it. runEndReason (derived, no schema bump) ends the run on maxed hunger/thirst or terminal infection; availableActions returns [] and sceneOf narrates the death. Starting kit (2 water,2 food) buffers the opening; eat/drink/treat gated to relevant turns (no clutter). Client shows the ending and stops. 12 engine + 3 harness tests. Engine 146 + harness 39 + content-loader 9 green; schema gate green. Strictly on the loop (no M2 world reactivity). Prepares the M1 Loop-Feel Check re-run.

tasks.title: Time-of-day danger
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-05: engine/src/sim/timeOfDay.ts fills the worldSim timeOfDay layer. One phase-danger owner: phaseConcealment (T15 detectChance sources it, behaviour-preserving), phaseSearchNoise (night searches deposit extra noise via the T14 params.noise override — noiseOf untouched), and a diurnal globalThreat tide (night 55 .. midday 15) the layer relaxes toward. tasks.title: Route conditions
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-05: engine/src/sim/routes.ts + new GameState.routes (SAVE_SCHEMA_VERSION 3->4, migrateV3toV4 seeds routes:{}). Undirected routes keyed by sorted node pair carry 0-100 wear -> clear / costly(+1h) / flooded(+2h) / blocked(not offered). Wear rises fast under weather movementDelta + low roads + fire and recovers slowly (hysteresis). Ticked as a stage-8 world effect after weather and in advanceWorld; availableActions consumes it (cost + availability + label). 11 tests + 1 migration test.

tasks.title: Apocalypse Director — pacing bias without impossible states
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-05: engine/src/sim/director.ts fills the worldSim director layer. Bounded bang-bang pacing controller: reads pressure (globalThreat blended with region threat) + player distress and nudges the current region density/threat by a clamped +-1 — escalate when calm (answers PL-M2-03), relief when overwhelmed, hold between. Never leaves 0-100 (property-tested), deterministic, gated by world.flags['director.disabled']. DoD proven via T32: director on vs off moves pacing metrics, both stay legal. 8 tests.

tasks.title: Living History — append-only world log
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-05: engine/src/sim/history.ts records into the existing GameState.history (no schema bump). One diff-based observer emits notable events (weather turn, nightfall, horde move, route condition change, cleared fight, run end), append-only, stamped with the resolved clock. Selective so it never makes the FR-CORE-04 audit vacuous. Wired at pipeline stage 13 (evaluateStory, threading the turn's before-snapshot) and in advanceWorld. 9 tests.

tasks.title: Telemetry instrumentation baseline
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-05: engine/src/telemetry/pacing.ts. samplePacing(state) is a pure client-driven snapshot of the PRD-4 proxies (pressure, tide, region threat/density, walker/horde load, blocked routes, distress, director on); summarizePacing folds a run into pacing metrics (mean/peak pressure, high/calm turns, oscillations, longest calm streak). Off by default — nothing in the pipeline captures. Deterministic over a seeded run; proves the T30 DoD. 4 tests.

tasks.title: Trust & disposition model
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-06 (M3 Part 1): src/sim/trust.ts — trust 0-100 on NPCState seeded from disposition (DISPOSITION_TRUST); asymmetric action deltas (help +15/share +10/trade +5; threaten -20/rob -30/abandon -25) via applyTrustEvent; adjustTrust clamps; trustTier bands + canParley (>=20) / canRecruit (>=70) gates feeding T35/T36. No free regen — trust never drifts, a betrayal sticks (property test + end-to-end). Pure/integer, save-lossless. FR-NPC-02 (VS subset).

tasks.title: Claim a shelter
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-06 (M3 Part 3): src/sim/shelter.ts — FR-SHL-01. Claim a node you have SEARCHED CLEAN (searchPct>=100) as your base while holding none — one active shelter per run — via a claim-shelter Scene verb wired into availableActions/sceneOf. Sets the reserved Player.shelterId (NO schema rung — the T3 shape, populated like actors were in T36), costs CLAIM_COST=4h, appends shelter.claimed to the Living History, and is a resolved player-changing turn (FR-CORE-04, no no-op). Payoff: resting at the base recovers extra fatigue (applyShelterRest in stage 4 — base +15, scaling with fortification). shelterLine reads the base into the Scene in plain text (NFR-ACC). Inert on shelterId===null, so every prior run stays byte-identical.

tasks.title: Fortify & upkeep
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-06 (M3 Part 3): src/sim/shelter.ts — FR-SHL-02. Fortify the base you stand in: spend one item.scrap (T17 loot tables) + FORTIFY_COST=3h to raise the reserved NodeState.barricades by FORTIFY_GAIN=25 (cap 100). Barricades decay FORTIFY_DECAY_PER_HOUR=1/h in pipeline stage 6 (updateNode) — a live upkeep decision, only ever touching a node with barricades>0 so prior runs are untouched; logs shelter.fortified on a genuine rise. Three integer payoffs scaled trunc(max*barricades/100): noise muffle at the base (SHELTER_NOISE_MUFFLE_MAX=20 → also RESISTS HORDE DRIFT since the T26 re-path reads node.noise), a detection floor in stimulusAt (SHELTER_DETECT_FLOOR_MAX=40 → dormant by day at full fortification, a stalker-at-night reduced NOT nullified), and deeper rest (SHELTER_REST_FORT_MAX=15). No schema rung; no new RNG; 14-stage order unchanged.

tasks.title: Shared stash
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-06 (M3 Part 4): src/sim/stash.ts — FR-SHL-03/FR-PLR-04. A base warehouse separate from the T18 carry budget. Deposit/withdraw offered only while standing in your own shelter (atOwnShelter), one choice per carried/stashed non-unique stack, FREE (0h, mirrors the T18 drop) — they change player.inventory<->player.stash without advancing the clock. The stash is WEIGHTLESS while stored (inventoryWeight reads inventory only), so a run banks surplus and weight still bites on the road; withdraw is gated on fits(). depleteStash(state,count) is the contested-world RAID hook — peels units in stable (alphabetical) order via the shared removeStashUnits and logs a single stash.raided beat; T40's cold branch rides it. cacheRead surfaces the store in shelterLine (words, never a number). The block's ONE schema rung: Player.stash, v6->v7 — migrateV6toV7 appends stash:[] (idempotent), SAVE_SCHEMA_VERSION=7; empty on every prior run so all are inert + byte-identical. 12 new engine tests (test/stash.test.ts) incl. the v6->v7 migration + a deposited round-trip; integer-only, save-lossless.

tasks.title: First authored story arc
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-06 (M3 Part 4): src/sim/story.ts — FR-STORY-01. 'The Last Customer' (arc.rivermouth.the-last-customer, subject npc.ruth): a deterministic trigger chain over the sim, NO save-schema rung (rides reserved-and-inert story.progress[arcId] as the int beat counter + queue for the delayed consequence — the same populate-a-reserved-shape move T37/T38 made). Opt-in per run: startRun gains arcIds, registerArcs seeds story.progress, so a run that enables no arc — and every prior golden — is byte-identical (evaluateArcs/resolveDueStoryEvents/storyChoices are true no-ops on progress{}/queue[]). Beats: DORMANT->PLEA (auto, stage 13, when you hold a shelter, have met her, and her T33 needs crossed needThreshold=60 at your base) -> HELPED/REFUSED (costed help/refuse via stage-3 dispatch: help spends stashDraw=2 + eases her needs + trust +25 and enqueues the good repayment; refuse trust -35 below parley — betrayal sticks — and enqueues the cold raid) -> RESOLVED_GOOD/COLD (stage 12, when due after delayHours=12: good caches repay supplies + trust; cold calls depleteStash(3) + barricadeHit 20 — the raided-stash story beat). Two identity pipeline stages graduate (12 resolveQueue, 13 evaluateStory) with names + 14-stage order unchanged. Authored content/arcs/arc.rivermouth.the-last-customer.json + content/schemas/arc.schema.json (gate 19/7); engine holds the authoritative VS dials, content mirrors them (harness integrity test guards drift). No RNG (pure predicates + fixed transforms) => byte-identical from seed; save-lossless (progress+queue round-trip); FR-CORE-04 clean (story/queue/history move). 14 engine tests (test/story.test.ts) incl. a subagent-found storyLine regression.

tasks.title: M4 decision gates — ADR-0004 + cross-run memory + licensing
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-16 (M4 Part 1): the three M4 decision gates resolved as written ADRs (PRODUCTION §9) so none stalls content/beta. ADR-0004 platform ordering — web-first RATIFIED as the sole launch surface through the M4 beta + M5 v1.0; the native/Steam/bot ORDERING formally deferred to the v1.0 launch gate (T69) with a named trigger (→ ADR-0004a), to be decided against beta telemetry not a guess; no second client built before v1.0 (WIP §6.6); interim lean (not a promise) is the bot as cheapest reach off the headless core. ADR-0005 cross-run memory (PRD Open Q6) — a bounded, local, per-profile Chronicle (endings + fallen/surviving survivors + a rolling window of Living-History beats, its own version + forward-only ladder) PLUS a capped enumerated set of light unlocks (starting-kit variants, seen recipes, cosmetic titles) chosen so none grants a mechanical head-start — the deterministic source M5 endings (T61/T62) read; retires PL-M2-06 for the cross-run layer; policy only, no engine code this part. ADR-0006 licensing (PRD Open Q8) — the M4 beta ships ALL-RIGHTS-RESERVED (the placeholder LICENSE stands as the real beta license, so the gate is closed and nothing stalls the beta); the final selection (open source / source-available / proprietary) is DEFERRED to the coupled T58 monetization decision with a named trigger, to be logged as ADR-0006a. Rationale: permissive licensing is a one-way door (shipped copies cannot be recalled), so the irreversible call is made AFTER the business model it constrains, not before it; T58 inherits a full option space and the modding/portfolio arguments for opening are recorded in the ADR to be weighed there. LICENSE unchanged. design/decisions/{0004-platform-ordering,0005-cross-run-memory,0006-licensing}.md authored + the README register updated; status ADR-0004 flipped to accepted and ADR-0005/0006 added. No code, no CI change.

Every node is content-as-data (FR-CNT-01): real five-question prose, a location kind (the FR-ECO-02 loot table), symmetric adjacency, per-run memory the engine seeds (searchPct/damage/corpses/blood/noise/fog/occupants/discoveries — persist all run, GDD VII), 14 claimable safehouses spread across the city, and walker/screamer/stalker seeding (the full roster is T46). Regions carry first-pass identity baselines (threat/density/loot/survivorActivity/power/water) — the M5 staged passes tune them. Authored via a validated generator so asymmetry, a dangling edge, or a disconnected node are impossible, then verified by the REAL engine buildRegionGraph (single start, symmetric, connected, known refs) + the schema gate. Rivermouth's six shipped nodes keep their exact prose/kind/flags — only city adjacency added — and the region keeps its loadContent-pinned baseline (threat 35, loot 70). An adversarial content-quality subagent audit ran (five-question test / kind fit / near-duplicates / accessibility / region identity): 5 fixes applied (ambulance-bay kind police→generic; a 'zombie dog' reframed to preserve the human-only undead canon; three motif de-duplications). content.test.ts's 5–8-node slice cap updated to the M4 city budget (40–65 nodes, 6 regions) and strengthened into a T44 guard (every region populated, cross-region routes exist, ≥5 safehouses). Engine tests read no shipped content, so the whole engine suite is untouched; the bigger world shifts Rivermouth's region-drift RNG from turn 2 (shared `region` stream), but every behavioral harness test holds. CI green in a clean sandbox: engine 349 · content-loader 9 · harness 48 · typecheck clean ×3 · schema gate 78 entries / 7 types · malformed rejected · empty-turn smoke exit 0. No engine code, no schema change, no RNG stream, no save-schema rung — pure content + one test-budget update. The §6.4 cap is now HIT (60/≈60): the signal to stop authoring breadth and go playtest before more M4 pours.

tasks.title: Full zombie roster
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-16 (M4 Part 2): FR-CBT-06/07 + FR-AUD-06. Completes the GDD §IX roster beyond walker/screamer/stalker with the four combat-distinct types — Fresh (swift: SWIFT_BONUS drives it to chasing from presence alone; initiative: answers EVERY melee exchange, not the walker coin-flip), Crawler (lowProfile: LOWPROFILE_DAMPEN reads it calmer than it is while you're only near, full the instant you stand on it; graspWound: a higher catch chance on a slip + an ankle sprain not a random blow), Bloated (burstInfection: killing it at your node by blow OR shot sprays a bite-severity infectious wound into the T22 track — the lesson is to slip past), Riot (armor: blunt strikes blunted to ~0 net, a firearm pierces — a bullet or a wide berth; hard, never a softlock since a slip is always offered + a firearm two-shots). Two schema extensions (zombie: signature/swift/lowProfile; enemy: armor/burstInfection/graspWound/initiative), the zombies.ts state machine (7-type ZOMBIE_BEHAVIOUR + swift/lowProfile wired into stimulusAt), and a type-aware combat.ts: an authoritative ENEMIES dial table (content/enemies mirrors it, a harness test guards drift), enemyForNode picks the most dangerous combat-distinct type present (priority riot>bloated>fresh>crawler, else walker — so screamer/stalker fight as walkers, the seam that keeps every pre-T46 fight byte-identical), armor/burst/grasp/initiative wired into resolveStrike/killEnemy/resolveEscape and combatNarration names the type + its signature. City re-seed: zombieTypes re-passed across 26 nodes in the 5 non-Rivermouth districts so each district's menace fits its identity (Riot at the fallen civic/police lines, Bloated through the hospital + waterworks, Fresh where crowds turned fast, Crawler in homes + machine floors), every combat-distinct node given walkers>0 so it's a LIVE threat not an inert tag (closes PL-M2-02 / PL-M4-02 for the seeded nodes); Rivermouth left unchanged (its marina stalker still inert by choice — PL-M4-10). tasks.title: Encounter categories, chains & multi-stage flows
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-16 (M4 Part 3): FR-ENC-03..08 · FR-CNT-03. The simulation now speaks in scenes via a DATA-DRIVEN encounter system — engine sim/events.ts is a generic interpreter over authored content/encounters/*.json (new encounter.schema.json), NO hard-coded per-encounter branching (FR-CNT-03). The pool is transient content on the graph (buildRegionGraph/startRun gain an opt-in list; empty ⇒ inert ⇒ every prior golden byte-identical). Closed vocabularies: requirement predicates (node/region/kind/phase, node-state bands searchPct/walkers/blood/corpses, humanity/mind, flags, requiresShelter, carriesItem, npcHere/metNpc, minStash) + effect verbs (setFlag/setRegionFlag/adjustHumanity/adjustTrust/adjustNeed/adjustMind/grantItem/takeItem/depleteStash/inflictWound/seedWalkers/addNoise/revealDiscovery/logHistory/advanceStage/endEncounter/scheduleFollowup). All six sub-reqs: categories(05, all 7); chains(03 — a flag set now read by a later requiresFlags, + a timed scheduleFollowup via the queue resolved stage 12); multi-stage(04 — advanceStage over the active encounter persisted in reserved player.quests, like a fight persists in combat; seedWalkers hands a stage to a real T15 fight — negotiation→fight→chase); moral→Humanity(06); false(07 — tension, no payoff); evolution(08 — three encounters sharing a node id gate on searchPct/walkers/flags → before/during/after). Selection by fit, deterministic (most-specific-then-id, NO RNG — weighting/cooldowns are T48), only on uncontested nodes, captures the turn, always offers an engine Step-away when authored choices are gated (no soft-lock). HUMANITY (owner chose a STORED scalar over a derived read): hidden player.humanity 0-100 (baseline 50, never a bar, felt only at extremes via humanityBand), logged as a moral beat for M5 endings (T61/T62) — the FIRST save-schema rung since v7: SAVE_SCHEMA_VERSION 7→8, one pure/total migrateV7toV8 (seeds humanity:50, a pre-moral run reads neutral); encounter machinery adds NO further rung. Content: a ~14-encounter proof set (owner chose over a larger pour) across all 7 categories — the garden-center evolution triple, the overpass-toll multi-stage, marina-cabin + the-scavenger morals (a real cost on every branch), two false beats (something-at-the-door, figure-in-the-window), a timed name-on-the-wall→the-one-who-scratched chain (burying the dead preserves Humanity), the-night-watch shelter beat, the-long-dark psychological, first-light, stripped-bare. playCli registers the pool; the golden-transcript generators deliberately don't (byte-stable). Adversarial content-quality subagent audit → 11 fixes (5 strictly-dominant 'obvious best' choices given a real time/resource trade, 3 moral branches given a cost on every axis, a mislabeled 'share' that granted no food, a rescue given a run-memory trace, 2 cadence tics); motivated the minStash requirement (gate 'pay the toll' on holding a can). CI green in a clean sandbox: engine 402 (+30) · content-loader 9 · harness 65 (+8) · typecheck clean ×3 · schema gate 115 entries / 8 types · malformed rejected · empty-turn smoke exit 0. New engine/test/events.test.ts + humanityMigration.test.ts + harness/test/events.test.ts. Deferred to T48: FR-ENC-01/02 tagged-pool weighting + cooldown suppression + the §4 verbatim-repeat-rate gate; Humanity's consumers (T61/T62 endings, T53 desertion) read it later. 14-stage pipeline order unchanged. See docs/plans/M4_PART3_PLAN.md + docs/qa/QA_REVIEW_M4_PART3.md.

tasks.title: Repeat-suppression & recombination (the §4 repeat-rate gate)
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-17 (M4 Part 4): FR-ENC-01/02 · FR-CNT-04 · the PRD §4 verbatim-repeat gate. T47's by-fit selection gains tagged-pool WEIGHTING + COOLDOWNS, so a full run keeps verbatim repeats under §4 (< 5%) by recombination + cooldowns, NEVER raw volume. Selection is now TIERED: an eligible one-shot (scripted) beat still wins deterministically by fit with NO RNG draw — a chain payoff / evolution beat is never randomized or crowded out — so every prior golden is byte-identical; only an all-repeatable eligible set runs the ambient model (a single candidate returns without drawing; ≥2 draw one weighted pick from a new named `encounter` RNG stream favouring FRESH (long since this id fired) + TAG-DIVERSE (long since a shared tag fired) content). The whole anti-repeat store rides the Living History: cooldowns + the §4 metric read the `encounter.begin` beats T47 already logs, so T48 adds NO save-schema rung (stays v8) and the `encounter` stream rides the existing open rng.streams map (lazily seeded) — the history scan is bounded to the schema's 336h cooldown cap. New content fields tags (FR-ENC-01 recombination/diversity handle) + weight on EncounterDef/encounter.schema.json; cooldownHours now ENFORCED. Instrumentation: engine/telemetry/repetition.ts (mirrors the T32 pacing baseline — pure, client-driven, off by default) folds the fires into verbatimRepeatRate (a same-id re-fire within a 48h recency window; cooldowns ≥ window drive it to ~0) + distinct/immediateRepeats/maxSingleShare/byCategory. Owner chose a HARD CI GATE (not a reported-only signal): harness/test/repetition.test.ts drives a deterministic full-run city sweep and asserts fires≥40, verbatimRepeatRate < 5%, immediateRepeats 0, distinct≥12, maxShare≤25% — MEASURED 134 fires / 54 days ⇒ 0.00% verbatim, 0 immediate, 20 distinct, 12.7% max share, across 5 categories (environmental 46/story 32/exploration 24/psychological 18/social 14 — the sweep holds nodes uncontested + claims no safehouse, so combat/shelter are out of its scope by construction; the gate asserts ≥4). Cadence rule added (applyAction): no NEW encounter opens on a turn spent resolving one, so the denser pool can't stack beats (inert for T47). Content (owner chose a ~10-14 demonstrator pool, NOT the launch pour): 12 tagged repeatable ambient encounters across 6 of the 7 categories (no ambient combat beat — encounters fire only on uncontested nodes per PL-M4-14, so the combat class stays the one-shot overpass-toll's) — city-voices ×3 (the-noticeboard/the-arrow/a-childs-schoolbag), the-dead ×2 (the-uncovered/the-note), living-thing ×2 (the-stray/birdsong), scavenger-sign ×2 (chalk-marks/still-warm), passing-stranger (across-the-street), holding-the-base (tending-the-base), night-nerves (the-small-hours) — each with cooldownHours ≥ 72 (> the window) + tags; FR-CNT-04 completed by bumping the two 2-choice anchors (a-name-on-the-wall, figure-in-the-window) to ≥3 approaches and the requiresShelter class to ≥3. Adversarial content-quality subagent audit → fixes applied (3 HIGH: two cost-free 'obvious best' branches + a dominated option whose label promised safety it didn't deliver, all restored to real trades; 5 MED: a weak/overpriced trade + three near-duplicate skeletons re-flavoured/re-valued to break the clone feel — the task's own point; LOW cadence tics/stock phrases). CI green in a clean sandbox: engine 418 (+16) · content-loader 9 · harness 75 (+10) · typecheck clean ×3 · schema gate 127 entries / 8 types · malformed rejected · empty-turn smoke exit 0. NO save-schema rung, 14-stage order unchanged. Retires the 'Content-hunger repetition' tripwire's measurement gap (the rate is now gated, not guessed). Deferred: the deep launch pool (extends PL-M4-16), a fire-probability cadence lever (PL-M4-18), narration-variant recombination (PL-M4-19); Humanity's consumers still later (PL-M4-15). tasks.title: Infection as staged identity
tasks.note: Done 2026-07-17 (M4 Part 5): FR-INJ-05/06/07/08 · GDD Part VI. Infection promoted from the T22 countdown-to-instant-death into a STAGED IDENTITY read only from symptoms — new sim/infection.ts holds the authoritative dials, survival.ts delegates the driver + run-end. Stages none→incubating(=GDD asymptomatic)→symptomatic→advanced→terminal; only 'advanced' is inserted (at progression 70) — the T22 symptomatic(40)/terminal(100) thresholds are unmoved, so every prior stageFor test holds. FR-INJ-08 (the crux): reaching TERMINAL no longer ends the run — it opens the playable cure race; the run ends by infection ONLY at a delayed succumb (progression ≥148, ~24h of continued untreated driver past terminal onset), so 'no instant Game Over' is literal AND 'some runs still end in death by infection' is preserved (the authored heightened death scene stays T62). Progression runs 0-148 (own clampInfection); the number is NEVER shown (FR-UI-02). FR-INJ-06: perception distorts at advanced/terminal — sceneOf injects hallucinated leads + (terminal) memory gaps via a STATELESS hash (new rng/prng.ts hashUnit over cyrb128) so a render stays pure, never advances the rng, and is resume-safe; framed as possibly-unreal, SUPPRESSED whenever a real danger lead is on the board (a hallucination can't sit beside/undermine a genuine horde), and availableActions keys off real state so it never fabricates a threat. The honest 'you can no longer trust your senses / tell what is real' warning rides the always-shown status at advanced AND terminal, so distrusting the story channel stays legible (fair). NPCs react to the visible sign; a visibly-dying (advanced+) player can no longer recruit (dialogue closes, encounters.ts). Stage-scaled fever fatigue (symptomatic 1/advanced 2/terminal 3 per hr) makes the run harder and compounds the succumb clock (more rest time = more driver). FR-INJ-07: infectionChoices (mirrors shelterChoices) offers diagnose (names the stage precisely, sets player.flags['infection.diagnosed'] — the opt-in clarity valve), treat-infection (antibiotics: strong+certain early, stage-scaled + SEEDED-uncertain at advanced/terminal via a new lazily-seeded 'infection' RNG stream — costlier + less certain the deeper it goes), and quarantine (shelter clean-conditions, strong early, NOT offered at terminal — no dead-option trap); each emits an honest no-number outcome (cleared/eased/held). All infection verbs gate on symptomatic+ (offering them while asymptomatic would leak the hidden clock; the asymptomatic lever is the ordinary bite treat). Content: new schema-validated type content/infections/ + infection.schema.json (gate 8→9 types) — infection.bite.json mirrors the engine's staged prose + cure/quarantine dials, guarded byte-for-byte by a harness drift test (the T40 arc pattern). ONE save-schema rung v8→v9 (migrateV8toV9): re-derive infection.stage from progression under the new bands (a v8 mid-infection save at progression 85 stored 'symptomatic' now loads 'advanced') — a value normalization, not a reshape; healthy saves untouched. Adversarial 2-subagent audit (engineering + design/comprehension) → fixes applied: quarantine-at-terminal dead-option trap removed, 'advanced' added to the director distress predicate (was skipped → a mid-stage difficulty dip), quarantine now earns the shelter rest bonus (was forfeited), hallucination suppressed beside a real threat, terminal carries the perception-warning, cure-outcome feedback added, comprehension gate strengthened (distinctness/escalation/no-number over status+scene+diagnosis/cure-removes-fever). CI green in a clean sandbox: engine 449 (+31) · content-loader 9 · harness 85 (+10) · typecheck clean ×3 · schema gate 128 entries / 9 types · malformed rejected · empty-turn smoke exit 0. Every prior bite-free golden byte-identical (all infection paths inert at stage none); 14-stage pipeline order unchanged. Retires the 'infection-as-identity is confusing' tripwire (comprehension-tested; the number is never shown). See docs/plans/M4_PART5_PLAN.md + docs/qa/QA_REVIEW_M4_PART5.md.

tasks.title: Radio network
tasks.note: FR-STY-03: the radio network — emergency / military / civilian / ham / unknown signals that evolve with world state (a station goes dark, a broadcast shifts as threat rises). Broadcasting and listening are priced risk per the M4 wireframe SCR-09 (Steel monopoly, aging signals). Content-as-data, deterministic from seed+state, save-lossless.

tasks.title: Economy — resource loops, spoilage, crafting, repairs
tasks.note: FR-ECO-04..07. Four resource loops — body / safety / health / power — each with drains and sinks (04); food spoilage, faster after power loss, and water purification (05); crafting (medical/weapon/shelter/survival) gated by blueprints, components, and rooms (06); and repairs that keep artifacts alive (07 — honest repair-over-replace per workshop wireframe SCR-10). Every option debits a real resource; deterministic; save-lossless.

tasks.title: Shelter depth — jobs & craftable rooms
tasks.note: FR-SHL-03/04, building on the M3 claim/fortify/shared-stash. Assignable jobs that run while the player is away, producing/consuming from the shared stash (03) — the base becomes a loop, not just a pack drain (closes PL-M3-01); and craftable rooms (kitchen/medical/workshop/radio/watchtower/garden) that unlock capability (04). Off-screen shelter upkeep lands here (closes PL-M3-05). Deterministic, save-lossless.

tasks.title: Factions & inter-NPC relationships
tasks.note: FR-NPC-02/05/06/07. Per-character memory driving per-relationship trust/respect/fear — not a global bar (02, Must); desertion and betrayal from low trust or mistreatment (05); dynamic conversations where a survivor's memories/knowledge act as real loot / location hints (06, Must); and inter-NPC friendships & rivalries that move shelter morale (07). The people-side off-screen sim (PL-M3-02) is a dependency.

tasks.title: Depth screens
tasks.note: FR-UI-04: on-demand depth screens — inventory, companions, shelter, map/journal, codex — layered over the story-first single-decision Scene without cluttering it. Keyboard-navigable and screen-reader friendly from the first cut (NFR-ACC-01/02); critical info never conveyed by colour or pointer alone; matches the M4 component-library states (default/pressed/focus halo/trust-locked).

tasks.title: Adaptive audio + non-audio equivalents
tasks.note: FR-AUD-01/02/06: a layered, state-driven adaptive mix — ambient/environmental/dynamic/player/music (01); audio as information — noise direction/distance, zombie-type signatures, heartbeat with Fear (02); and a non-audio equivalent for every meaningful sound cue (06, Must). The game must be fully playable with sound off — accessibility, not polish.

tasks.title: Difficulty modes & accessibility baseline complete
tasks.note: Difficulty modes in — survivability/scarcity/pacing dials, calibrated later in the M5 staged balance passes; and the NFR-ACC baseline completed from its M1 start (NFR-ACC-01..04): text-first with no colour/audio-only cues, keyboard-only, screen-reader navigable, one-hand mobile layout. Accessibility is a Must from the first UI and finished here — never an M5 retrofit.

tasks.title: M4 exit — content-complete city & public-beta hardening
tasks.note: The M4 Definition of Done. The first city is content-complete and schema-valid (CI green, FR-CNT-02); verbatim encounter repetition sits under the PRD §4 target across a full run; infection-as-identity plays as 'a harder way to keep going', comprehension-tested with players; and the beta is stable enough to hand out. A pass opens M5 (release candidate). This milestone is the MVP / public beta.

tasks.title: Monetization & business-model decision
tasks.note: PRD Open Question 5 (PRODUCTION §9 decision schedule, resolve by M5): decide the monetization / business model — premium / free+cosmetic / episodic — so launch planning can finalize. A written decision against its criteria, logged like an ADR. Licensing (Open Q8) was already settled at the M4 beta gate (T43); this closes the last open commercial decision before v1.0.

tasks.title: Staged balance pass 1 — survivability & scarcity
tasks.note: GDD XVI / PRODUCTION §8 staged balance, passes 1–2 (survivability → scarcity). Tune death timing / causes / margins and resource scarcity against the M2 telemetry baseline (§4 proxies), never by feel — every change justified by telemetry or a logged playtest and re-checked against the Survival Triangle (no strategy escapes a trade). Begins retiring the balance-fragility risk (§10).

tasks.title: Staged balance pass 2 — pacing/director & difficulty modes
tasks.note: PRODUCTION §8 staged balance, passes 3–4 (pacing/director → difficulty modes). Tune the Apocalypse Director's escalate/relief bands (T30) and the M4 difficulty dials (T56) against pacing telemetry (mean/peak pressure, oscillations, longest calm streak); director + comeback mechanics carry the tension. Verify no mode yields an impossible or trivial run. Completes the balance-fragility retirement (§10).

tasks.title: Multiple endings from run components
tasks.note: FR-STY-06 (Must, v1): assemble multiple endings from real run components — who lived, what you built, the choices logged in the append-only Living History (T31) — with no single 'true ending'. Reproducible from seed+state; the history is the source, so an ending reflects the run that actually happened.

tasks.title: Failure endings with closure + epilogues
tasks.note: FR-STY-07 (Must) + FR-CBT-10 (Last Stand) + FR-STY-08 (Should). Authored failure endings — Last Stand / overrun / infection — resolve as a heightened final-choice sequence with real closure, not a death card; plus epilogues following surviving NPCs. Death is a scene. Builds on the M3 first authored failure ending (T40/T42).

tasks.title: Full accessibility — verified with assistive tech
tasks.note: NFR-ACC-01..04 completed and VERIFIED with real assistive tech, not just authored: critical info never by colour/audio alone (01), full screen-reader support over a semantic, navigable text UI (02), scalable text + high-contrast / colorblind-safe themes (03), reduced-motion / reduced-flicker modes (04, Should). Accessibility has been a Must since the first UI (M1); here it is proven, never retrofitted.

tasks.title: Localization externalization complete
tasks.note: NFR-LOC-01 (Should): all player-facing strings externalized for localization, completed to 100% (the design kept the art-bible §3.7.7 +35% expansion headroom). No hard-coded copy in the shipped path; a pseudo-locale pass proves nothing is baked in. Actual translations may follow post-launch, but externalization ships at v1.

tasks.title: Save migration proven across a real schema change
tasks.note: NFR-SAVE-02 (Must): prove the versioned-save migration ladder (ADR-0003 / T7) across a REAL breaking schema change — an old save from an earlier SAVE_SCHEMA_VERSION loads, migrates forward one pure N→N+1 rung at a time, and resumes losslessly with no silent corruption. The vertical slice already bumped the version to 7 with per-bump rungs; M5 proves the whole ladder end-to-end on a shipped-format save.

tasks.title: Reliability & performance hardening
tasks.note: NFR-REL-01 + NFR-PERF-01/02 (Must). Crash-free session rate ≥99.5% at launch with zero save-corruption — the deterministic core gives repro-from-seed for every report; and hold the turn budget: resolve+render < 100 ms on a mid-range 2022 phone, initial load < 3 s, heavy region/horde updates amortized (addresses PL-M2-07). Soak-test long runs and bound the append-only history growth (PL-M2-06).

tasks.title: Polish & UI-restraint pass
tasks.note: FR-UI-06 (Should): the emotional-UI and restraint pass — the 'Quiet Screen' for loss moments (SCR-08 register), a UI that degrades legibly with player state, and a final cut of anything that clutters the story-first single-decision Scene. Gated on art/audio direction locked (M5 entry). The perceivable finish that makes v1 feel authored, not prototyped.

tasks.title: Content freeze & every-Must-met release audit
tasks.note: The M5 DoD backbone: freeze content, then audit that every Must requirement is met at its release target (PRD §7 MoSCoW) — no Must cut to hit the date; if one can't fit, the milestone is re-scoped, not shipped broken. Schema gate green over the full city (FR-CNT-02); the Won't-now register stays frozen; every shipped item traces back to the PRD by ID.

tasks.title: v1.0 launch gate — the external 'one more day' test
tasks.note: The M5 exit and v1.0 go/no-go. Balance targets hit in telemetry; a save survives a real migration; accessibility verified with assistive tech; and the 'one more day' test (GDD XVI) passes with EXTERNAL players, not just the owner — at session end they want to continue. A pass ships v1.0; native/Steam/chat-bot clients and any second city move to Post-launch per the ADR-0004 order.

adrs.title: Save storage & versioning
adrs.blocks: Unblocked: local-first client-owned saves + T7 forward-only migration ladder accepted 2026-07-05; cloud sync deferred post-launch

adrs.title: Platform ordering (native/Steam/bot)
adrs.blocks: Resolved 2026-07-16 (M4): web-first ratified as the sole launch surface; the native/Steam/bot ordering deferred to the v1.0 launch gate (T69) with a named trigger → ADR-0004a. Beta unblocked.

adrs.title: Cross-run memory persistence
adrs.blocks: Resolved 2026-07-16 (M4): a bounded, local per-profile Chronicle + a capped set of light unlocks (no mechanical head-start) — the deterministic source for M5 endings (T61/T62). Unblocks endings & meta-progression.

adrs.title: Licensing (proprietary/source-available/open)
adrs.blocks: Resolved 2026-07-16 (M4): the beta ships all-rights-reserved (the placeholder LICENSE is its real license); the final license selection is deferred to the coupled T58 monetization decision under a named trigger (ADR-0006a). Unblocks public-beta distribution without walking the one-way door early.

risks.how: Slice Fun Gate PASSED 2026-07-06 (M3) — the slice produces a retellable, systemic story end-to-end. Risk retired.

AI-at-volume behind review-capacity cap; the verbatim-repeat rate is now MEASURED + hard-gated (< 5% across a full run, T48) via tag/condition recombination + cooldowns, not raw volume. Fully retires at M4 exit (T57) once the launch pool is poured behind the gate.
Cut filter + Won't-now register + parking lot

tripwires.response: Force the time-boxed decision; pick and move

tripwires.tripwire: New idea enters a live milestone
tripwires.response: Route to Parking Lot; triage at boundary only

tripwires.tripwire: Build unplayed > 1 week, or 3+ systems half-done
tripwires.response: Stop; drive one to done; play it

parkingLot.note: [ADDRESSED in M2 Part 2 — scene-surfacing pass] Surface the reactive world in the Scene (weather, rising threat/density, an approaching horde, a roused/screaming node) — required before the M2 'feels alive' fun-gate can be judged. Highest-value next M2 step.

Couple the T25 zombie state machine + types to combat/encounters (zombieState biases danger; Screamer/Stalker fight differently). Give type-only nodes a body — the marina Stalker currently has walkers:0 and is inert in play.

[ADDRESSED in M2 Part 2 — T30 director escalation bias, T24 the neutral substrate] Off-screen drift de-escalates the opening region (threat 35->7 over 7 idle days), against the 'land pressure by turn 3' exit note. Add an escalation bias (natural home: the T30 Director), keeping T24 as the neutral substrate.
Loop advanceWorld hour-by-hour for off-screen fast-forward (step-based layers don't scale with hours); season-gate weather; wire weather noiseFactor into the T14 noise model.

Combat slip/retreat still ignore route conditions (they use SLIP_COST, not the route wear) — deliberate so a fight can never strand the player behind a blocked road. Revisit if fleeing should also cost/deny by route.

Living History is append-only and unbounded — a very long run grows the save. Add a rolling window / summarisation (a client concern per DESIGN, or an engine cap) before long-session soak; faithful to 'never rewritten' today.

Per-turn world cost now also scans every route (T29) and diffs before/after for history (T31), on top of the Part-1 O(nodes+regions+hordes). Fine at Rivermouth scale; watch against the phone budget as the map/horde count grows (the known heavy path).

Companion upkeep is pack-only — a companion's needs are relieved solely by the player's give-food/give-water from a finite pack, so a supply-poor run loses them to attrition (intended, but a standing drain with no self-feeding). Give companions/survivors a base to draw from when the shelter + shared stash land (T37/T39) so keeping people is a base loop, not only a pack drain.

No off-screen people-sim and survivors don't move — npcs/companions drift only on the player's turns (stage 5), not inside advanceWorld, and non-companion survivors stay pinned to their node. A survivor left starving in a district you fast-forward past doesn't die until you return. The people side of stage 10 (moveGroups) + off-screen upkeep is a later M3 block (overlaps PL-M2-04 for the world half).

Model cleanups before the 60-100 survivor pool: a recruited Survivor carries no name in state (recoverable from content via type; engine companion prose is generic 'your companion'), and dead survivors linger in npcs unbounded as remembered bodies. Add a name on the reserved shape (or a client lookup) and a corpse sweep.

Interaction depth deferred: threaten only spends trust (no intimidation payoff — info/forced trade), and recruit reads trust+met only (no disposition check, no party cap — a hostile-disposition survivor is recruitable, party size unbounded). Wire threaten-for-something and recruitment guards when the party grows past the one-companion VS.

Off-screen shelter upkeep deferred — barricades decay only on the player's turns (pipeline stage 6, updateNode), not inside advanceWorld, so a base abandoned for days is not weaker for it (kept that way to preserve byte-identity this block). Land off-screen barricade decay alongside the deferred off-screen people-sim (PL-M3-02).

No attacker on the base yet — a fortified shelter resists detection + horde drift but nothing assaults it (no night-raid/siege event, no raided-stash beat; the stash itself is T39). The payoff is currently inferred from the ABSENCE of danger, not survival of an attack. Dramatise it with the T39/T40 contested-world hooks.

Shelter VS simplifications to revisit: fortification reuses the generic NodeState.barricades (untangle if node-generic barricading ever ships); claim is once-per-run with no relocate/abandon verb; fortifying is silent (safe-base fantasy over hammering-draws-the-dead); and costs/rates (CLAIM_COST 4, FORTIFY_COST 3, FORTIFY_GAIN 25, decay 1/h, the three *_MAX payoffs) are first-pass dials for the T42 Fun Gate / M5 balance.

The cold raid is a narrated stat beat (depleteStash + barricadeHit resolved off a rest in stage 12), not a played, moment-to-moment defense of the shelter — the drama is inferred from the aftermath (cache/barricade reads change), not survived live. Extends PL-M3-06: the live night-siege/defended-base fight is still deferred (M4 contested-world). The T40 raid is the first thing that ATTACKS the base, but off-turn.

One authored arc, one subject (Ruth): the slice tells a story well but a second run tells the SAME story. Retellability ACROSS runs — the real target of the 'one more day' test — is unproven by a single arc (the arc LIBRARY is post-gate M4 content by design). This is the central open question the Fun Gate (T42) must weigh with real playtesters.

Story VS simplifications to revisit: the arc's help branch is gated on the cache covering stashDraw, so a bare cache at plea time leaves refuse the only resolution (intended for the VS 'she came back for what you would not give', but a 'you have nothing to give' third path is a lever); the arc is authored engine-side (src/sim/story.ts, THE_LAST_CUSTOMER) with content/arcs mirroring the dials + carrying the surfaced prose (VS content/engine bridge, harness-guarded against drift) — reading dials FROM content is post-VS; the companion payoff (help Ruth -> recruit gate) is available but not surfaced as the arc's reward; and all arc dials (needThreshold 60, delayHours 12, stashDraw 2, raidUnits 3, barricadeHit 20, helpTrust +25, refuseTrust -35) are first-pass, tuned at the T42 Fun Gate / M5 balance.

The 6-kind loot enum (generic/store/medical/police/residential/industrial) is coarse for a full city — a fire station and a military/armory node both fall to `police`, a hospital ambulance bay to `generic`. Fine at city-VS scale; revisit a richer kind→loot-table mapping (or a per-node loot-profile field) when the T51 economy and the T17 tables grow.

Node zombie seeding is limited to walker/screamer/stalker (the M2 roster) until T46 ships the full type set; several city nodes carry these three as first-pass menace. Re-pass zombieTypes across the city when T46 lands so each district's signatures match its identity (FR-CBT-07).

Region baselines (threat/density/loot/survivorActivity/power/water) and the 60-node distribution are first-pass identity dials, not balanced against a real cross-city run — the M5 staged balance passes (T59/T60) move them; the M2 telemetry over the bigger map is the input.

Inter-region travel is not surfaced as anything special yet — a gateway route reads like any other move. Which district am I entering and what is it known for is depth-screen / map-journal work (T54) and the fog/journal pass; author it so crossing a region boundary reads.

The city is authored but UNPLAYED at scale, and the §6.4 node cap is now hit (60/≈60) — the PRD's explicit signal to STOP authoring breadth and go playtest. A real traversal playtest of the full city (pacing across regions, does the drift/director read at city scale, is 60 nodes too many to hold in the head, do the gateways route well) should precede further M4 content pours.

Adding regions shifts Rivermouth's region-drift RNG from turn 2 (the shared `region` stream now draws once per region per tick), so multi-turn region values over shipped content differ from the M1–M3 slice — expected and harmless (all behavioral tests hold), but any FUTURE golden that pins exact region numbers must be regenerated against the city, not the slice. If per-run reproducibility of Rivermouth's drift across content growth is ever wanted, seed the region jitter per-region-id rather than sequentially.

Companions don't fight yet — a companion follows/holds/scavenges/guards but does not participate in combat (FR-NPC-03 autonomy remainder); the T46 type-aware combat is player-vs-dead only. Wire companion combat behaviour (competence/morale/fear) with the fear/panic model (FR-CBT-09) and deeper companion AI.

Companion orders tick only on the player's turns — scavenge banking + guard barricade upkeep happen in pipeline stage 5, not off-screen (extends PL-M3-02/05). A companion left on hold/scavenge in a district you fast-forward past isn't simulated until you act. Land with the off-screen people-sim.

First-pass companion-order dials, untuned against a real run (M5 balance T59/T60): PARTY_CAP 3, ORDER_TRUST_MIN 80, SCAVENGE_HOURS_PER_UNIT 2 / SCAVENGE_EXTRA_DRAIN 2, GUARD_UPKEEP_PER_HOUR 1, COMPANION_SHARE_TRUST 8. Scavenge banks a fixed item (item.canned-food); a richer yield table follows with the T51 economy.

Rivermouth's marina stalker is still inert (walkers:0) — left that way deliberately to protect the slice/Rivermouth goldens this part (the rest of the M2-roster inert nodes were made live in the re-seed). Give it a body when a Rivermouth-touching golden refresh is next due.

Pool composition to watch as it grows toward 60-100: at 18 the cast skews to care/veteran-professional roles (3 medical-adjacent + the exemplar paramedic) and mid-career ages. Widen the age band and add more trades/young/other backgrounds in later authoring passes, still under the review-capacity cap.

The human voice/casting pass is still owed — the characters cannot be auto-approved. This part drafted + self-reviewed them and ran an adversarial subagent audit (11 fixes applied); the owner's voice pass before beta is the real gate.

T48 owns the anti-repeat gate. Selection this part is deterministic-by-fit; the tagged-pool weighting, cooldown suppression, and the §4 verbatim-repeat-rate instrumentation (FR-ENC-01/02) are the next part. The proof-set encounters are one-shot or state-gated so they don't spam without cooldowns; ambient/repeatable encounters wait for T48.

Encounters fire only on uncontested (walkers==0) nodes — a deliberate seam so the active-encounter branch never shadows the T15 walker prompt and an engaged encounter can't be walked away from mid-flow. The cost: encounters can't surface WHILE the dead are present at a node. Revisit if a 'the fight interrupts the negotiation' beat is wanted inside one node.

Humanity has no consumers yet — the scalar is tracked and felt (prose bands ≥85 / ≤28 / ≤12) but nothing reads it for a gate. The intended readers are ending assembly (T61/T62), companion desertion / refusal-to-stay (T53), and how the world's story remembers the player. Wire them when those land.

First-pass encounter dials + a ~14 proof set, not the launch pool. Humanity deltas, need/mind costs, walker counts, delay hours, and the band thresholds are untuned against a real cross-city run (M5 balance T59/T60). The deep pool that makes a full run rarely repeat is the content pour, gated behind T48's repeat-rate signal + the review-capacity cap; the owner's human voice/casting pass on the encounter prose (as for characters, PL-M4-12) is still owed before beta.

revealDiscovery writes bare content ids into node.discoveries (e.g. disc.gc.drawing) with no content/discoveries type or schema behind them yet — the prose lives in the encounter, the id is a hook for a future codex/journal (T54) + environmental-storytelling content. Author the discovery entries when the depth screens land.

Encounter CADENCE is uncapped: on a quiet node an ambient fires whenever one is eligible (T47 behaviour), so the fire RATE self-regulates only via cooldowns (a burst, then quiet as the fresh pool goes on cooldown, then a refresh). T48 adds no fire-probability gate (its scope is which content fires + not repeating, FR-ENC-01/02, not how OFTEN). A per-turn fire-chance lever (some quiet nodes are just quiet) is a natural M5-balance addition; the new same-turn anti-stack rule (no new encounter opens on a resolution turn) is the only cadence guard so far. First-pass ambient dials (cooldowns 72-120h, weights default, the 48h recency window, mind/humanity deltas) are untuned against a real cross-city run (T59/T60).

The DEEP launch pool is still owed (extends PL-M4-16): T48 ships a ~12 tagged repeatable demonstrator that PROVES the mechanism holds the < 5% verbatim gate, not the launch-scale breadth. The deep pour (dozens more encounters so a full run rarely repeats even unwindowed, and every district/kind has its own fitting ambient set) is the content work behind the T48 repeat-rate signal + the review-capacity cap + the owner's voice/casting pass (PL-M4-12). A future 'narration-variant recombination' lever (one encounter, several interchangeable prose renderings) could stretch a compact pool further than tags/conditions/evolution alone — not needed to hit the windowed gate here.

The AUTHORED terminal death scene is deferred to T62 (M5 failure endings — 'death is a scene', a heightened final-choice sequence with real closure). T49 leaves the succumb seam (runEndReason 'infection' at progression ≥148) and a plain death narration ('The fever crested and did not break…'). When T62 lands, replace that plain ending with the authored Last-Stand-style infection close, reading the run's Living History (incl. the new infection.staged / infection.treated beats).

Infection SOURCES are bite-only for now. The staged machine (sim/infection.ts) is source-agnostic and the schema's source enum already lists wound/tainted-water/spoiled-food/blood — the GDD's non-bite sources (deep wounds in filth, infected blood, tainted water, spoiled food) get their own content + drivers with the T51 economy/spoilage. Latent: the driver keys on WOUND_EFFECTS[type]==='infect-risk' (today only the exact id 'wound.bite'); a future content-authored bite with a subtyped id (e.g. 'wound.bite.forearm', the Wound.type doc example) would not drive infection until WOUND_EFFECTS keys it. Combat inflicts the exact 'wound.bite', so it fires today.

First-pass infection dials, untuned against a real cross-city run (M5 balance T59/T60): thresholds symptomatic 40 / advanced 70 / terminal 100 / succumb 148, BITE_INFECT_RATE 2/h, stage fatigue 1/2/3 per h, cure amounts 50/50/(30 full·12 partial)/(18·4) with certainty 100/100/70/40 %, quarantine 30/30/10/0. The fever-fatigue lever is a real but INDIRECT difficulty (fatigue is non-fatal + has no combat/awareness malus; its systemic reader is the director's comeback relief) — the real teeth are the succumb clock + distortion + failing recruit/cure costs; if 'harder' needs more bite later, give fatigue an awareness/accuracy malus above a threshold. Diagnose is near-free (holds a med item, 1h, consumes nothing) — the clarity valve; a later pass could cost a charge / gate on a medical skill or EMT companion, and restore the plan's clinic-node diagnosis path (cut here for a graph-free gate).

Adjacent health/mind requirements T49 deliberately did NOT touch: the Mind model (FR-INJ-09 — stress/fear/hope/morale surfaced as behaviour/dialogue/dreams, not bars) is a separate requirement; T49 uses only fatigue as infection's consequence and leaves Mind.stress/morale untouched. Companion/community infection — spread in the shelter, quarantine's trust/morale strain ('isolates the sufferer and strains trust', prose-only here), contagion from wound.illness — needs the people-side off-screen sim (PL-M3-02) + factions T53. Scars persist and NPCs reference them (FR-INJ-11, Could/v1) later. A within-terminal ramp (memory gaps intensifying as succumb nears) is a possible polish lever, not built.

loopFeelCheck.build: M1 complete (T10–T22); engine 146 + harness 39 tests green
loopFeelCheck.moment: Turn 7 of a live playthrough: 'You are thirsty and tired' surfaced a Drink water (1h) option — the first real resource+time trade; the survival clock makes move/search/rest a live decision it wasn't before T22.
loopFeelCheck.decision: Loop grips enough to proceed; M2 (world reactivity) is the correct next investment, not more loop tuning.
entryNotes: Opening ~day still too calm — pressure should land by ~turn 3 (start needs non-zero or trim starting kit).
Decisions are maintenance-level until scarcity bites; M2's contested world (rivals, hordes on noise) supplies the dilemmas.
